resources.string: To use your braille display for typing, go to input settings to turn it on
To continue typing, set the TalkBack braille keyboard as your input method
To switch between contracted and uncontracted, press space and dots 1, 2, 4, and 5.
Turn on braille on screen overlay
Turn off braille on screen overlay
To use braille display, first turn on TalkBack
To use braille display, first turn on Bluetooth
To use braille display, %s needs access to nearby devices
You need to enable \"Location\" permission for %s before you can use braille display
To use braille display, %s needs access to your device’s location
To change your typing language, press %s at the same time
To change your reading language, press %s at the same time
Make sure this braille display is turned on and nearby.\n\nIf it still won\'t connect, double-tap \"Learn more\" for other troubleshooting suggestions.
Switch to enhanced keymap to use browse mode
To use browse mode, you\'ll need to switch from the default keymap to the enhanced keymap. This change will update some of your braille display commands

These braille display commands were updated in this version of TalkBack:

• Previous item: Press dots 1, 7

• Next item: Press dots 4, 8

• Previous reading control: Press dots 2, 3, 7, 8

• Next reading control: Press dots 5, 6, 7, 8
To quickly switch between contracted and uncontracted using a braille display, press space and dots 1, 2, 4, 5.\n\nThis command works if you’re using a language with both braille grades.
Learn key commands for your braille display
Use navigation keys to move the focus on the screen. Use the braille keys to enter text in a text box.\n\nBelow are all the commands supported by your device.
Shortcuts for moving between web content such as headings
Learn how on-screen elements are shown on your braille display
Elements on your screen are represented by the following abbreviation symbols on your braille display
• ⠿⠄ represents an element that you can activate
• ⠿⠤ represents an element that you can touch and hold
• edt represents an editable text field
• mln represents a text field with multiple lines
• () or (x) represents a checkbox or radio button
Parentheses or x in parentheses represents a checkbox or radio button
• h%1$s to h%2$s represents heading %1$s to heading %2$s
• [grd 2r 1c] represents a table with 2 rows and 1 column
• [r1 c2] [grd 5r 6c] represents currently at row 1 and column 2, in a table with 5 rows and 6 columns
Learn commands available on your braille display
Connect a braille display to learn commands
Press a refreshable braille key to learn its function. Press control with w or escape to exit.
No display found, stopping learning mode. Please connect Braille display and relaunch learning mode.
Automatically advance the braille display at a set speed
Your braille display can advance at a set speed so you can read without pressing any buttons. To turn auto-scroll on or off, press space and dots 1, 2, 4, 5, 6
To increase the display duration, press dot 4. To decrease the display duration, press dot 1.\n\nThese commands only work when auto-scroll is turned on.
Display duration changes based on text length
Allow saved braille displays to connect when nearby
Reverse pan up and pan down buttons
Show device messages, like app notifications and incoming calls, on the braille display
Press space and dots 1, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8 to get a list of recent calls, app notifications, and more
Can’t connect to braille display. Try again.
Move navigation focus forward or adjust reading control up
Move navigation focus backward or adjust reading control down
Top of page or beginning of text
Bottom of page or end of text
Turn TalkBack speech on or off
Switch to the next typing language
Switch to the next reading language
Select from cursor to beginning of text box
Select from cursor to end of text box
Activate enter key when editing text
These commands are only available while editing text

You can navigate web pages quickly by using the command to enter browse mode, then typing the following letter or number shortcuts\n\nThese shortcuts can also be used without entering browse mode, but you\'ll need to add dot 8 to the \"next\" commands
You can turn it on again anytime in TalkBack settings
Commands for navigation, braille settings, and some TalkBack features aren’t available while typing or editing.\n\nTo use these commands, first close the keyboard.
To show or hide captions for this video, press space and dots 1, 4, 7, 8.\n\nTo automatically show captions for supported media, turn on Show captions on braille display in Settings.
Always show this message when captions are available
Your %s\'s battery is charging your braille display.\n\nIf you need to switch to Bluetooth, disconnect your USB or USB hub and go to TalkBack settings > Braille display.

Your %s's battery is charging your braille display.

To save battery:

 1. Go to braille display settings
 2. Disconnect your braille display from USB
 3. Connect to your braille display using Bluetooth
This will store some messages, like notifications and alarms, in TalkBack. You can change this setting anytime.
